projectDescription: CASR (Crash Analysis and Severity Report) is a suite of CLI tools and a Rust library for collecting, analyzing, triaging, and estimating the severity of crash reports from sanitizers, GDB, fuzzers, and multiple programming languages.
keyFeatures: Crash severity estimation (EXPLOITABLE / PROBABLY_EXPLOITABLE / NOT_EXPLOITABLE) for x86, x86_64, ARM32, AArch64, RISC-V architectures
Report collection from AddressSanitizer, MemorySanitizer, UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer, and GDB
Multi-language crash analysis: C/C++, Rust, Python, Java, JavaScript, C#, Go, Lua
Crash deduplication and clustering based on stack trace comparison
Automated triage pipelines for AFL++, libFuzzer, LibAFL, and Sharpfuzz
SARIF report generation for IDE integration
DefectDojo integration for vulnerability management via casr-dojo
LibCASR Rust library for programmatic crash analysis
publicApiSurface: casr-san — generate reports from AddressSanitizer/MemorySanitizer output
casr-python — generate reports from Python crashes
casr-java — generate reports from Java crashes
casr-js — generate reports from JavaScript crashes
casr-csharp — generate reports from C# crashes
casr-lua — generate reports from Lua crashes
casr-cli — TUI viewer; joint statistics; SARIF export
casr-afl — automated triage for AFL++/Sharpfuzz crash directories
casr-libfuzzer — automated triage for libFuzzer/LibAFL crash directories
casr-dojo — upload unique reports to DefectDojo
libcasr::stacktrace — stacktrace parsing, filtering, deduplication, clustering
